summary: Frontend-only e2e testing of the editorial portfolio (Home + Journal + Blog Post). 8/9 user flows pass. One CRITICAL HashRouter incompatibility bug: in-page anchor links (href starting with '#' or '/#section') replace the HashRouter route hash and navigate to the NotFoundPage instead of scrolling to the section.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking 'See the work' (data-testid='hero-cta-work') uses <a href="#work">, which under HashRouter replaces the current '#/' route hash with '#work'. This matches route '*' and renders the 404 NotFoundPage instead of scrolling to the #work section. URL after click: '/#work', screen shows 'Nothing here, yet.' 404 page.

nav items defined with to='/#work', '/#about', '/#contact' are rendered as plain <a href> via NavItem isHash branch. In HashRouter, navigating to '/#work' sets URL hash to 'work' (no leading slash) and routes to NotFoundPage. Reproduced: clicking 'Work' nav link from / -> 404 page. Same issue for About and Contact links.
Footer.jsx Sitemap renders plain <a href='/#work'> and <a href='/#about'>. Same HashRouter conflict — these will land users on the 404 page rather than scrolling to the in-page anchors.

action_items: Fix HashRouter + hash-anchor incompatibility. Recommended: implement onClick handlers that call document.getElementById('work').scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' }) and use preventDefault() instead of href='#work' / href='/#work'. Apply to hero-cta-work, Header NavItem hash branch (Work/About/Contact), Footer sitemap Work/About links, and journal-preview-section #contact references if any. Alternatively switch to BrowserRouter (but then github-pages deep links break) or use react-router-hash-link.
Verify after fix that clicking Work/About/Contact on Header/Hero/Footer scrolls smoothly to the corresponding section without navigating to NotFoundPage.
critical_code_review_comments: HashRouter + in-page hash anchors are fundamentally incompatible. Header.jsx NavItem.isHash branch generates broken links by design.
BlogPostPage.jsx fetch uses process.env.PUBLIC_URL — verified working (markdown loaded and parsed with frontmatter + remarkGfm + rehypeRaw). No issues.
NotFoundPage is catch-all '*' route which is correctly silently absorbing the broken hash links — making the bug user-visible as a 404 instead of a no-op.
All required data-testid attributes are present and selectors work reliably.
Mobile menu auto-closes on route change (useEffect on location.pathname) — good UX.

success_rate.frontend: 78% (7/9 user-listed flows fully pass; in-page anchor scroll flow broken)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All blog flows (list, post markdown rendering with heading/p/blockquote/code/list, back link, missing slug fallback, hero->journal link, header Journal link, mobile menu open with 5 nav links, footer mailto:hello@example.com) are PASSING. The only outstanding issue is the HashRouter + '#section' anchor incompatibility that affects hero-cta-work and Header/Footer Work/About/Contact links. After main agent fix, re-run only the hero-cta-work scroll test and Header Work/About/Contact nav clicks to confirm scroll-to-section without 404. App public URL: https://clean-folio-29.preview.emergentagent.com/ (HashRouter URLs: /#/, /#/blog, /#/blog/<slug>).
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: react-router-dom's HashRouter uses the URL hash to encode its route (e.g. '#/', '#/blog'). When a child component renders a plain <a href='#work'> or <a href='/#work'>, the browser replaces the entire hash portion with 'work' (or '/#work' rewrites to '#work' after relative resolution). HashRouter then sees pathname 'work' (no match) and routes to '*'/NotFoundPage. Fix: replace anchor-based scrolling with programmatic scrollIntoView, or wrap with react-router-hash-link's HashLink which preserves the route hash. Reproduction steps: (1) Open /#/, (2) click 'See the work' or header 'Work' nav, (3) URL becomes /#work, (4) 'Nothing here, yet.' 404 page is rendered.
